_comment: Blind quality ratings (1-10) per run from independent reviewer agents.

00.justification: Metadata serialization (base64+JSON) and network exclusion correctly implemented with round-trip robustness and backward-compatible fallback, but lacks JSON-serialization error handling and tests.

01.justification: Correct metadata extraction/consumption via pickle sidecar, but breaks backward compatibility by removing model transfer with no fallback for old configs lacking the metadata file.

02.justification: Sound core JSON design but critical missing test update (EXCLUDED_FROM_INI) causes a test failure that would block merge.

03.justification: Correct JSON-in-config fix with backward-compatible fallback; minor concern is no error handling if metadata is not JSON-serializable.

04.justification: Comprehensive fix: extracts metadata to JSON, writes to config, consumes in generation, stops network transfer, with solid backward-compatible fallback.

05.justification: Well-implemented torch.save sidecar carrying metadata through config; removes network transfer and keeps backward compatibility; minor style/robustness refinements.

06.justification: Well-executed compact-JSON fix; properly integrates with bilby_pipe parser, clean fallback, minimally invasive.

07.justification: Correctly embeds JSON metadata in the complete config, eliminating the network transfer while keeping backward compatibility; edge-case handling for missing metadata could be more defensive.

08.justification: High-quality fix using str(dict)+ast.literal_eval with robust round-trip, backward-compatible fallback, and correct titled-group placement; not verified in integration.

09.justification: Core fix correct and complete, but no error handling for missing metadata and no backward-compatibility fallback; JSON safety claimed but unverified.

10.justification: Core fix correct and avoids network transfer, but critically omits updating EXCLUDED_FROM_INI in test_parser_ini_consistency.py, causing a test failure.
